compiled_state.title: As a developer, I can run the add-parameter recipe on TypeScript codebases with cross-file caller resolution
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add-parameter recipe for TypeScript via ts-morph, with cross-file caller resolution using the TS compiler reference graph.
**Why**: Extends the add-parameter recipe to TypeScript — the second language implementation, proving the engine adapter interface works across engines.
**Scope**:
- IN: add-parameter recipe for TypeScript via ts-morph, cross-file caller update via TS compiler reference graph, ts-morph engine adapter implementing S1's documented interface contract
- OUT: Python add-parameter (S1), scaffold-route (S3), normalize-imports (S4), Ruby recipes

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a developer can run add-parameter on a TypeScript project and it updates the function signature plus all callers across files via ts-morph's reference graph
  ← Satisfies: "Initial catalog includes add-parameter (cross-file signature + caller update)"
- When this story is complete, running add-parameter twice on the same TypeScript input produces identical output, and 3 consecutive runs produce zero-variance output
  ← Satisfies: "Each recipe is idempotent" and "Determinism: 3 consecutive runs produce zero-variance output"
- When this story is complete, the ts-morph engine adapter conforms to S1's documented engine adapter interface
  ← Satisfies: cross-story interface contract
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Must implement against S1's engine adapter interface — not reverse-engineer the Python/Rope path
- [Testing] Cross-file resolution depends on tsconfig.json project setup — test with both single-project and composite-project configurations

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
